app.name: Google Photos Deduper
app.one_liner: A Chrome extension that finds and removes duplicate photos from your Google Photos library using local image embeddings.

screens.name: Deduper Dashboard
screens.purpose: Display duplicate photo groups and allow user to select which copies to delete.

rust_commands.name: move_to_trash
rust_commands.purpose: Remove selected duplicate photos from the user's Google Photos library by interacting with the web interface via GPTK.

rust_commands.name: scan_library
rust_commands.purpose: Fetch user's Google Photos media items and compute local MediaPipe embeddings to detect visual duplicates.

mvp_plan: week 1: Build UI for duplicate review and selection
week 1: Implement Google Photos Toolkit (GPTK) interaction layer
week 1: Integrate MediaPipe for local image embedding generation
acceptance_tests: Extension successfully scans Google Photos library without 403 Forbidden errors
Installation via unpacked Chrome extension works as documented
TensorFlow/MediaPipe duplicate detection runs without runtime errors
User can review duplicate groups and move selected items to trash
